title: Tie Meshtastic text payload limit to app-interface max
description: Reviews for project-LICHEN-t2hn.7.1/.7.2 noted CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_MAX_PAYLOAD is set to 200 for Meshtastic compatibility while the protocol limit is LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_TEXT_PAYLOAD_MAX. Add a compile-time assertion/shared constant or other no-hardware guard so the two limits cannot drift silently.
notes:
Implemented a compile-time equality guard in lichen/apps/gateway/src/meshtastic_adapter.c: BUILD_ASSERT(CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_MAX_PAYLOAD == LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_TEXT_PAYLOAD_MAX). This scopes the invariant to the Meshtastic gateway app-interface handoff, where Kconfig sets the app-interface payload max to the Meshtastic protocol limit, without imposing the Meshtastic 200-byte limit on generic app-interface-only builds.

Explorer: Mencius mapped CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_MAX_PAYLOAD defaults (generic 176; gateway Meshtastic override 200) and LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_TEXT_PAYLOAD_MAX=200, and recommended this exact guard in the gateway Meshtastic adapter as the smallest no-hardware drift check.

Code reviews: Singer found no issues and confirmed includes/build scope/Kconfig matching. Wegener found no issues and confirmed equality is better than >= because it catches drift in both directions while leaving app-interface-only tests unaffected. Helmholtz found no blocking issues and identified one low coverage gap: no automated negative compile-fail fixture that intentionally mismatches the constants. Follow-up project-LICHEN-t2hn.7.3.1 was created for that negative compile-fail coverage. No P0/P1/P2 findings.

Local checks: git diff --check passed.
